title: Uinta High Lakes
subtitle: Mirror Lake Highway, Utah
summary:
## In and Up

First real overnight with both boys carrying their own gear. They handled it. The Lofty Lake Loop is the friendliest entry point into the High Uintas — moderate climb, lakes everywhere, and you can string together a few nights without ever covering more than three miles in a day.

We got to Reids Peak trailhead around 10am and were at our first camp by early afternoon. Set up between two stands of pine, twenty feet from the water.

## Fishing

The brookies were hungry. Younger Bro caught his first fish on a fly — a small one but he wouldn't stop talking about it for the rest of the trip. Older Bro tied on his own setup by day two.

> Tip for next time: bring the lighter line. The 3wt was overkill on these little guys.

## The Storm

Night two we got hit with a real Uinta thunderstorm. Tent held up. Boys were rattled but proud they made it through. Hot chocolate and a card game in the vestibule turned it around.

## Lessons for Next Trip

- Pack one less dinner — we came home with extra food every time
- Bring a real backpacking chair, the camp stools are miserable
- Boys can carry more than I think
- Always pack the deck of cards
